methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - news.vcu.edu
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional commitment to distributed AI literacy in Richmond, creating peer networks beyond elite tech hubs—a key ingredient for broad-based capability building rather than platform dependency.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates grassroots capability development in a historically underserved region, testing whether deep skills can take root outside traditional tech corridors through local ownership.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - pilotonline.com
signals.signal_reading: AI-powered home search tools for Hampton Roads real estate agents exemplify shallow, platform-delivered adoption—agents consume AI features without building underlying capability, spreading usage but not expertise.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding skill-building infrastructure signals displacement rather than empowerment, pointing toward shallow adoption that automates entry-level roles without creating new capability pathways.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI trainer's emphasis on workers leading technology in Roanoke/Southwest signals intentional deep learning over passive tool consumption, suggesting regional awareness that meaningful AI fluency requires active skill development, not just platform access.
